title: Fix react-hooks/rules-of-hooks violations in teacher_responsibility page
description: 9 conditional hook calls flagged by lint at src/app/.../teacher_responsibility/page.tsx lines 125-285. Conditional hooks corrupt React state across renders. Lint warns but does not block. P0 because violations sit in user-facing teacher responsibility flow. Action: refactor to call all hooks unconditionally at top, move conditional logic into hook bodies or render guards.
close_reason: Split into ClassroomResponsibilityPage wrapper + ClassroomResponsibility child. 11 rules-of-hooks warnings eliminated. Lint 35→25 warnings, typecheck clean, 733/733 tests pass. Commit 9beddac6.

title: E2E suite red on main: schedule-config nav, program-mgmt, publish-gate
description:
E2E (parallel + sequential) jobs failed on main since 2026-05-27 across 3+ commits. Failures span unrelated specs:
- e2e/03-schedule-config.spec.ts TC-007-01: Navigate to configuration page (30s timeout, 3 retries)
- e2e/09-program-management.spec.ts TC-PROG-050: heading structure
- e2e/16-publish-gate.spec.ts: should prevent publishing of incomplete semester
- Plus many '⚠️ No draggable elements found' warnings throughout

Lint hotfix in PR #223 did not touch these specs and was unrelated. Bug is pre-existing.

Symptoms look like navigation timeouts on dashboard pages and possibly seed or app startup drift since 2026-05-27. Investigate first: 03-schedule-config TC-007-01 timeout (most consistent). Run pnpm test:e2e locally to repro.
design:
Investigation 2026-05-29:

ROOT CAUSE: Tests pre-redesign, pages mid-redesign. Not a single regression — chronic drift since UI refactors started.

EVIDENCE:
- Schedule config: e2e/03-schedule-config.spec.ts TC-007-01 expects Thai labels 'กำหนดคาบต่อวัน', 'กำหนดระยะเวลาต่อคาบ', etc. None of those strings exist in src/ — page was refactored from form to ConfigSummaryClient (read-only summary) when wizard absorbed initial config (commit 4e26f832 on 2026-05-27).
- Public schedule pages: e2e/public-schedule-pages.spec.ts uses selectors 'td .font-medium', '.text-xs' assuming <table>-based layout. 4a0 redesign plan Tasks 6-7 migrate to TimeslotGrid (<div>-based card layout). Selectors won't survive migration.
- Orphan files: src/app/(public)/teachers/[id]/[academicYear]/[semester]/ has 3 variants (page.tsx + page-new.tsx + page-grid.tsx) — partial migration started outside the official plan.
- Visual snapshots: pre-redesign baselines.

REMEDIATION PATH (recommended):
- Do NOT do a big-bang test rewrite.
- Scope new E2E coverage under each migration task in the 4a0 plan:
  - Task 6 PR (public teacher schedule → TimeslotGrid): rewrite public-schedule-pages.spec.ts teacher tests + clean orphan page variants.
  - Task 7 PR (public class schedule): rewrite class portions of public-schedule-pages.spec.ts and public-data-api.spec.ts.
  - Task 8 PR (dashboard all-timeslot): rewrite e2e/smoke/semester-smoke.spec.ts dashboard tests + critical-ui.spec.ts dashboard snapshots.
  - Tasks 11-13 PRs: rewrite TeachersTableClient / ClassesTableClient list tests.
- Separately (not 4a0 scope): rewrite e2e/03-schedule-config.spec.ts to test ConfigSummaryClient read-only summary view. File as new bug. The current spec was correct for the old form, but that UI no longer exists.

DO NOTS:
- Don't 'fix' tests by relaxing selectors generically — that just hides the next regression.
- Don't regenerate visual snapshot baselines until the redesign is complete (Task 14+).
- Don't merge any orphan page-*.tsx variants without claiming them in a task PR.
acceptance_criteria: E2E parallel + sequential jobs green on main and PR branches
notes:
Local repro 2026-05-28: 330 pass / ~50 fail / 180 skip / 11 didn't run in 39.1 min.

Failure clusters:
1. Schedule config nav (smoke + visual + critical-smoke)
2. Public schedule pages (public-data-api.spec.ts, public-schedule-pages.spec.ts)
3. Visual snapshots (schedule-config, lock-schedule, teacher-arrange, export)
4. Performance benchmarks (page load timings)
5. Public class/teacher schedule pages

CHRONIC, not fresh: gh shows E2E last green on main 2026-04-06 (~2 months ago). Red since approximately April. Matches '4a0 public + dashboard tables redesign' plan scope — tests likely pre-redesign, code post-redesign. Will be addressed as part of that 16-task plan (likely Tasks 8+).

Decision: Task 1 (subject-color util, PR #222) does NOT make this worse. Lint hotfix #223 was correct unblocker. Defer E2E green requirement until redesign plan reaches relevant tasks.

title: Remove email service (no mail server)
description: User has no mail server. Remove ACS mailer, EmailOutbox queue, email-outbox admin UI + cleanup cron, and email-sending wiring in better-auth. Keep email/password LOGIN; drop password-reset + email-verification + email-change flows (all require sending mail). Drop email_outbox table + EmailOutboxKind/Status enums via migration. Regenerate Prisma client (resolves C-1 emailOutbox build-break). Remove @azure/communication-email dep.
close_reason: Email service removed: deleted mailer.ts/test, @azure/communication-email dep (-13 pkgs), features/email, management/email-outbox UI, cron route; stripped auth mail wiring (sendResetPassword/emailVerification/changeEmail/outbox); dropped EmailOutbox table+enums via migration 20260526120000; fixed breaktime enum drift in schema; regenerated client. Kept email/password login. typecheck OK, 768 tests pass.

title: Fix N+1 in public-data.repository getRoomOccupancy/getPeriodLoad
description: Public homepage MiniCharts triggers ~40-55 sequential prisma counts (1 per timeslot in getRoomOccupancy + 1 per weekday in getPeriodLoad). Each round-trips remote Accelerate -> dev hangs/slow page load. Replace with single findMany + in-memory tally and Promise.all parallelization.
close_reason: Fixed in 10ca9791 (N+1 collapse) + b5bc90ea (local docker DB swap)

title: E2E test env leaks PRISMA_DATABASE_URL from .env, routes seed/tests at remote DB
description: prisma/seed.ts:76 `import "dotenv/config"` auto-loads .env, which sets PRISMA_DATABASE_URL pointing at the remote Prisma Postgres / Accelerate database. prismaClientSingleton() in src/lib/prisma.ts prefers PRISMA_DATABASE_URL/ACCELERATE_URL over the locally-set DATABASE_URL when present, so e2e seeds and the test web server were silently running against the remote production-like DB. Symptom: seed fails with USER_ALREADY_EXISTS_USE_ANOTHER_EMAIL even though local test DB is empty (`docker exec timetable-test-db psql ...` returns 0 rows in User). Fix: add empty PRISMA_DATABASE_URL= and ACCELERATE_URL= overrides to .env.test and .env.test.local. Follow-up: consider gating the Accelerate selection in prismaClientSingleton on NODE_ENV !== 'test' to make this trap impossible.
close_reason: Fixed in 483dd8f0: explicit empty PRISMA_DATABASE_URL/ACCELERATE_URL in .env.test prevents .env leakage from routing seeds at remote Prisma Postgres. 12/12 teaching-assignment e2e specs now pass.

title: Cleanup and re-seed prod database
description: Run seed:demo on production database to refresh data.
close_reason: Local seed redone with cleanup. Prod seed remains user-triggered (destructive op needs explicit confirmation).

title: Remove 'as any' on Prisma groupBy in assign + dashboard repos
description: Two live-code type escapes bypass Prisma type safety: src/features/assign/infrastructure/repositories/assign.repository.ts:106 and src/features/dashboard/infrastructure/repositories/dashboard.repository.ts:351 both cast prisma.<table> as any to call .groupBy(). Brittle against Prisma upgrades. Action: investigate why typed groupBy fails (missing select? Prisma client regen needed?), fix root cause, drop casts.
close_reason: Dropped (prisma.model as any).groupBy() in both repos. Root cause: as T[] result cast triggered backward inference through Prisma InputErrors conditional type. Fix: fully inferred return type, removed unused local type aliases.

title: Decide fate of 2 stranded WIP auth-migration stashes
description: Two large stashes stuck on stale base commits (0e9451b, fa95b33). Both contain ~12k LOC churn: Better Auth migration, AGENTS.md rewrite, schema.prisma changes, password migration scripts, proxy.ts.old. Risk: stashes rot against current main; conflicts compound daily. Action: review each, land partial work as PRs or drop with note. stash@{0}=editable table+teacher CRUD+E2E. stash@{1}=subject credit type guards + auth migration.
close_reason: Dropped stash@{0} (auth migration superseded, Better Auth already live). Dropped stash@{1} (subject credit + mgmt pages on stale base). Salvaged credit-titles.ts mapped type fix before dropping.

title: Clean up orphan public teachers schedule page variants (page-new.tsx, page-grid.tsx)
description:
src/app/(public)/teachers/[id]/[academicYear]/[semester]/ contains 3 page variants:
- page.tsx (current production route)
- page-new.tsx (orphan)
- page-grid.tsx (orphan)

Only page.tsx is the active App Router route — the others are unreachable artifacts from an aborted or in-flight migration. They drift independently from the live page, confuse search/code-review, and risk being accidentally promoted.

Resolution: either roll their useful work into Task 6 (4a0 — migrate public teacher schedule to TimeslotGrid) or delete them as part of that PR. Don't merge them via separate cleanup PR — Task 6 will rewrite this directory anyway and clean delete is part of the migration.

Parent context: school-timetable-senior-project-8kp. Block Task 6 work on resolving this.
acceptance_criteria: Only page.tsx remains in src/app/(public)/teachers/[id]/[academicYear]/[semester]/ after Task 6 merges.

title: Rewrite e2e/03-schedule-config.spec.ts vs ConfigSummaryClient (post-wizard refactor)
description:
The schedule config page at /schedule/[year]/[sem]/config was refactored from an interactive form to a read-only ConfigSummaryClient when the wizard absorbed initial config (commit 4e26f832, 2026-05-27).

The existing E2E suite e2e/03-schedule-config.spec.ts still drives the old form: it asserts Thai labels 'กำหนดคาบต่อวัน', 'กำหนดระยะเวลาต่อคาบ', 'กำหนดเวลาเริ่มคาบแรก', 'กำหนดคาบพักเที่ยง' and interacts with form inputs (e.g. 'ตั้งค่า' button). None of that UI exists on /config anymore.

This bug is NOT in 4a0 redesign scope — the config page change predates 4a0. Filed separately so the rewrite can land on its own PR rather than waiting on Task 14.

Scope:
- Rewrite TC-007 tests to verify ConfigSummaryClient renders the saved config (days, periods, durations, breaks) as a read-only summary.
- Move any 'configure-from-scratch' coverage to the wizard E2E (already exists per commit 094183dc clr).
- Delete tests that asserted form-only behavior (reset, mini-break inputs, etc).

Parent context: school-timetable-senior-project-8kp.
acceptance_criteria: e2e/03-schedule-config.spec.ts passes against current ConfigSummaryClient. No new flakiness in green tests.

title: Public + dashboard tables redesign (4 tables)
description: Spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-28-public-tables-redesign-design.md. Redesign student list, teacher list, all-program, and weekly timeslot grid across public landing and dashboard. Modern-minimal direction, card+sidebar lists, category-band program, subject-hash timeslot grid sharing TimeslotGrid component. Consumes break v2 (BreakDefinition[] + isBreakForGrade/Teacher). No schema change.

title: Step 2 curriculum & MOE check panel
description: NEW presentation-only panel for wizard step 2. Per grade/program, show required subjects + periods from program_subject vs MOE_WEEKLY_STANDARDS; run validateProgramMOECredits (src/features/program/domain/services/moe-validation.service.ts) live and surface shortfalls BEFORE any placement. Read-only summary with links to fix program/subject master data. No new domain logic — compose existing program + MOE services.
acceptance_criteria: Step 2 shows per-program MOE pass/shortfall. A deliberately under-credited program (e.g. M4 Science-Math short 2 periods) is flagged here AND blocks publish in step 6. pnpm typecheck/lint/test green.
close_reason: Step-2 curriculum/MOE panel delivered. Pure buildCurriculumOverview (curriculum-overview.ts, 4 unit tests) maps active programs -> per-program MOE view (Thai track label, total/required credits, per-learning-area current/required/deficit, errors/warnings) + allValid aggregate; delegates math to validateProgramMOECredits. curriculum/page.tsx server component: findFullConfigData(configId=sem-year) -> overview -> read-only cards + top pass/fail banner + 'แก้ไขหลักสูตร' link to /management/program; empty-state when no programs. Verified: typecheck clean, my files lint clean (3 pre-existing errors unrelated), wizard suite 16 pass, build exit 0 (curriculum route compiled). Surfaces shortfall EARLY; publish-blocking half is existing checkPublishReadiness (2qw). Live browser render deferred to u21 E2E (auth+seed).

title: Whole-school auto-solve orchestration (headline action, step 4)
description: NEW server action (or POST /api/schedule/auto-arrange-all) that auto-generates the entire school timetable in one pass. Loads all teachers for the term, orders most-constrained-first, calls the existing greedy solve() (src/features/arrange/domain/auto-arrange) per teacher accumulating placements, writes in ONE $transaction, returns aggregate stats + unplaced/failures[] list. Powers the headline button 'สร้างตารางอัตโนมัติทั้งโรงเรียน' in wizard step 4. Wrap in createAction (auth+admin RBAC+Valibot) or mirror the existing /api/schedule/auto-arrange admin gate.
design: Reuse solve() as-is; no new algorithm. Teacher-ordering heuristic (most-periods-first vs most-conflicted-first) decided empirically against seeded data during impl. Expose failures[] so admin finishes leftovers in step 5 regardless of ordering choice. Single transaction for atomicity (all-or-nothing per run).
acceptance_criteria: Calling the action on a seeded term fills the grid for all teachers conflict-free. Returns {placements, failures[], stats}. Admin-gated. Transactional. Unit/integration test on seeded data.
notes:
DONE (orchestration + endpoint). New files:
- src/features/arrange/domain/auto-arrange/whole-school-solver.ts — pure solveWholeSchool(): orders teachers most-constrained-first (sum remaining periods desc), calls solve() per teacher feeding each teacher's placements forward as ExistingSchedule (teacherId-keyed lane — NOT pendingPlacements, which is single-teacher) so no cross-teacher room/grade double-book. Returns {success,placements,failures,perTeacher,stats}.
- __tests__/whole-school-solver.test.ts — 6 unit tests (empty, independent-aggregate, cross-teacher room conflict, cross-teacher grade conflict, most-constrained-first ordering, per-teacher outcomes). All green; full auto-arrange suite 46 pass.
- src/app/api/schedule/auto-arrange-all/route.ts — POST {academicYear,semester}, admin-gated, single $transaction persist. Scopes responsibilities by AcademicYear+Semester (per-teacher route only filters TeacherID).
- index.ts barrel exports added.
Verify: typecheck clean; new files lint clean (3 pre-existing lint errors in TeacherAssignmentPage.tsx/timeslot/break-utils are unrelated, dn3 territory).
DEFERRED to shell (fyy)+btq: the headline button UI 'สร้างตารางอัตโนมัติทั้งโรงเรียน' wiring. Route-level integration assertion folded into E2E u21 (needs docker pg seed).
close_reason: Orchestration core delivered + unit-tested (solveWholeSchool, 6 tests, feed-forward conflict avoidance + most-constrained-first ordering + aggregate stats) and POST /api/schedule/auto-arrange-all endpoint (admin-gated, single transaction). typecheck clean, new files lint clean, auto-arrange suite 46 green. Headline button UI wiring deferred to fyy (shell-dependent); route E2E execution covered by u21.

title: Wizard shell + step gating (replace tab hub in schedule page)
description: Replace the free-navigation tab hub in src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/page.tsx with a forward-gated stepper reusing the CreateSemesterWizard pattern (src/app/dashboard/_components/CreateSemesterWizard/). 6 steps wired (config, curriculum/MOE, assign, auto-generate, review, lock&publish). Gating computed from existing state: config exists? responsibilities exist? grid filled? Mirror configCompleteness/getPublishReadiness logic in src/features/config. Block forward-skips only; allow revisiting completed steps.
design: This is the spine of the epic. Each step hosts/routes to existing surfaces. Step 3 routes to canonical /management/teacher-assignment (i8z). Step 5 mounts the existing arrange parallel routes. Keep deep-link/back-nav working.
acceptance_criteria: Wizard renders all 6 steps with correct gating. Cannot open arrange before a grid exists. Revisiting completed steps allowed. pnpm typecheck/lint/test green. UI checked via browser_eval MCP.
notes:
SHELL IN PROGRESS. New feature src/features/schedule-wizard/:
- domain/wizard-steps.ts — pure: WIZARD_STEPS (config,curriculum,assign,generate,review[arrange],publish[lock]), resolveStepAccess, isStepReachable, furthestReachableStep, FIRST_STEP. Forward-cumulative gating from {hasGrid,hasResponsibilities,hasPlacements}; revisit allowed. 12 unit tests green.
- application/services/wizard-state.service.ts — server-only getWizardState(year,sem): counts timeslot/responsibility/class_schedule for term.
- presentation/components/WizardStepper.tsx — client MUI nonLinear Stepper; current step from usePathname; StepButton disabled when unreachable; useEffect forward-skip guard redirects to furthestReachableStep.
Wired: layout.tsx now renders persistent header (title+เปลี่ยนภาคเรียน, relocated from page.tsx) + <WizardStepper>; base page.tsx rewritten server-side → redirect to furthest reachable step. Placeholder segments curriculum/ (2bv fills) + generate/ (btq fills).
Verify: typecheck clean, lint clean (new+changed), full suite 786 pass. Build running. Browser/E2E verification (auth+seed) deferred to u21.
NOT DONE in this slice: step-4 generate button wiring to /api/schedule/auto-arrange-all (target noted; needs generate page real impl), publish step content (2qw). Old base page.tsx Tabs removed.
close_reason: Wizard shell + gating delivered. Pure gating (wizard-steps.ts) 12 unit tests green incl 'arrange blocked before grid' + 'revisit allowed'. getWizardState server service + WizardStepper client chrome wired into layout.tsx (persistent header relocated from page.tsx); base page.tsx → server redirect to furthest reachable step; placeholder segments curriculum/+generate/. Verified: typecheck clean, lint clean (new+changed), full suite 786 pass, production build exit 0 (all 6 step routes compiled). Live browser_eval render (needs authed admin + seeded term) folded into E2E u21 which depends on this. Step content = 2bv(curriculum)/btq(generate+review)/2qw(publish); generate-button target /api/schedule/auto-arrange-all noted.

title: Teacher scheduling flow redesign (guided wizard, by-teacher, auto-first)
description:
Replace the free-navigation tab hub at /schedule/[academicYear]/[semester] (page.tsx, default tab 'assign') with a forward-gated guided wizard, reusing the CreateSemesterWizard stepper pattern. Fixes three pains captured this session: confusing step order, two competing assign flows, too much manual placement. MOE compliance (Matthayom M1-M6, src/config/moe-standards.ts) becomes a hard constraint surfaced EARLIER (step 2), not only at publish.

6 wizard steps, each gating the next:
1. Config grid (existing /schedule/.../config + table_config)
2. Curriculum & MOE check (program_subject vs MOE_WEEKLY_STANDARDS, validateProgramMOECredits live)
3. Assign teachers (canonical /management/teacher-assignment by-teacher; reuse epic i8z work, NOT re-implemented here)
4. Auto-generate whole school (NEW orchestration over greedy solve())
5. Review & tweak (existing arrange grid + per-teacher auto-fill via /api/schedule/auto-arrange + conflict resolver)
6. Lock & Publish (readiness gate: checkPublishReadiness)

Plan file: C:/Users/napat/.claude/plans/goofy-seeking-coral.md. Approved via ExitPlanMode 2026-05-27.
design: Approach A (forward-gated wizard) chosen over alternatives. Gating computed from existing state (config exists? responsibilities exist? grid filled?), mirroring configCompleteness/getPublishReadiness in src/features/config. Forward-skip blocked; revisiting completed steps allowed. Reuse: CreateSemesterWizard stepper, solve() + POST /api/schedule/auto-arrange (already auth+admin+transactional), MOE services, createAction wrapper, conflict-resolution.actions. Out of scope: MOE standard values, ConfigID/TimeslotID formats, Prisma schema, new solver algorithm, email re-add, Verification-model drift.
acceptance_criteria: One linear gated workflow that cannot land users in broken states. Whole-school auto-generate is the headline action. Legacy /schedule/.../assign* redirects to canonical hub (via i8z/lyw). MOE shortfall blocks publish AND shows in step 2. pnpm typecheck/lint/test/test:e2e green. E2E walks wizard end-to-end on seeded term.
notes: Step 3 (assign teachers) is delivered by existing epic i8z (canonical /management/teacher-assignment by-teacher mode): dn3 in-progress, lyw=legacy redirect, 7xb=delete legacy. Wizard step 3 routes to that hub — do not re-implement assignment here. Tried hard dep bzc->i8z but bd rejects epic-blocks-epic in this config; treat as soft reuse dependency. Children: fyy (shell/spine, build first), r4t (whole-school orchestration, parallel), 2bv/btq/2qw (step panels, need fyy), u21 (E2E, needs all). lock.actions.ts:144-187 createBulkLocksAction transaction fix folded into 2qw.
close_reason: Core redesign delivered + E2E green (u21 7/7): forward-gated wizard replaces tab hub — config gate, step-2 curriculum/MOE panel, by-teacher assign, whole-school auto-generate headline, review grid + per-teacher autofill + conflict resolver, lock + publish readiness gate. Two P3 spin-offs (827, z45) remain open as standalone follow-ups.

title: Investigate codebase: write ARCHITECTURE, CODE_REVIEW, REFACTOR_PLAN, FEATURE_MAP docs
description: End-to-end Next.js 16 architecture audit per /investigate workflow. Deep pass scope: read every page.tsx, *.actions.ts, app/api/**/route.ts plus 8 named critical files. Output 4 markdown deliverables under docs/. No source code edits.
close_reason: Wrote 4 docs: ARCHITECTURE.md (system + 6 mermaid), FEATURE_MAP.md (top 5 journeys), CODE_REVIEW.md (29 findings: 3C/7H/11M/8L), REFACTOR_PLAN.md (3 phases). Deep pass covered 41 pages, 18 action files, 16 API routes, 8 critical libs. Critical: schema drift (EmailOutbox/Verification missing from schema.prisma), 2 unauthed mutating actions, 1 missing admin role check.

title: Phase A: feature-parity audit legacy /schedule/.../assign vs /management/teacher-assignment
description:
@
Audit deliverable for epic i8z. Produce capability matrix:

Legacy surfaces to inventory:
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/assign/page.tsx (AssignPageContent)
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/assign/component/ShowTeacherData.tsx
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/assign/component/QuickAssignmentPanel.tsx (298 LOC)
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/assign/component/AddSubjectModal.tsx
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/assign/component/SelectClassRoomModal.tsx
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/assign/components/LockedScheduleList.tsx
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/assign/hooks/useTeacherSchedule.ts
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/assign/hooks/useTeacherLockedSchedules.ts
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/assign/teacher_responsibility/page.tsx

Canonical surfaces:
- src/features/teaching-assignment/presentation/components/* (TeacherAssignmentPage, SubjectAssignmentTable, AssignmentFilters, TeacherSelector)
- src/features/teaching-assignment/domain/services/teacher-validation.service.ts

Output: markdown matrix in issue notes via bd update --notes, columns: [Legacy capability | Canonical equivalent | Gap (none/port/refactor) | Owner phase].

Acceptance:
- Every legacy capability classified.
- Gaps explicitly listed for Phase A port work.
- No code change in this issue.
@
notes:
Audit complete. KEY FINDING: legacy is NOT fully redundant with canonical.

| Legacy capability | Location | Canonical equivalent | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| Teacher search Autocomplete | ShowTeacherData.tsx:221 | TeacherSelector.tsx (grade-row context only) | port — canonical lacks top-level teacher picker |
| Teacher workload card (>22h warning) | ShowTeacherData.tsx:354 | teacher-validation.service warnings (row-level) | port — no aggregate card view |
| Subject stats (count by category) | ShowTeacherData.tsx:144 | none | port (or drop if unused) |
| Class stats (unique classes count) | ShowTeacherData.tsx:162 | none | port (or drop) |
| Locked schedule list per teacher | LockedScheduleList.tsx + useTeacherLockedSchedules.ts | none | port (dn3 covers) |
| Teacher-centric editor (per-classroom card per year) | teacher_responsibility/page.tsx:113 ClassroomResponsibility | none | gap — canonical is grade-centric only |
| Add classroom modal | SelectClassRoomModal.tsx | none | port if teacher-editor kept |
| Add subjects to classroom modal | AddSubjectModal.tsx | none | port if teacher-editor kept |
| syncAssignmentsAction (replace teacher's full set) | API action | bulkAssignTeachers (upsert per-row) | gap — different semantics (full-replace vs upsert) |
| Grade-centric editor (subject rows + teacher dropdown) | none | SubjectAssignmentTable.tsx + TeacherSelector | canonical exclusive |
| Copy from previous semester (per grade) | none | copyAssignmentsAction + handleCopyFromPrevious | canonical exclusive |
| Clear all assignments per grade | none | clearAllAssignmentsAction | canonical exclusive |
| Workload validation (per-row) | none | teacher-validation.service | canonical exclusive |
| AssignmentFilters (grade+sem+year picker) | none | AssignmentFilters.tsx | canonical exclusive |

REVISED EPIC SCOPE — three options:

A. Add teacher-centric mode to canonical. /management/teacher-assignment grows a tab/toggle: "by grade" (existing) vs "by teacher" (port legacy). Largest scope, fullest consolidation.

B. Keep teacher-centric flow but move under /management. Rename routes: /management/teacher-assignment/by-teacher (port from /schedule/.../assign/teacher_responsibility). Drops year/sem from URL path, uses query params. Mid scope.

C. Reduce scope: only collapse the grade-centric portion. /schedule/.../assign/page.tsx (ShowTeacherData) → redirect to /management/teacher-assignment. KEEP /schedule/.../assign/teacher_responsibility as-is or relocate. Smallest scope, doesn't fully unify.

RECOMMENDATION: Option B. Single management surface, two clearly-named modes. Legacy teacher_responsibility editor has real value (full-replace UX is faster for entering new teacher's full load).

NEXT: epic i8z owner picks option, updates sub-issues dn3/lyw/7xb scope, then proceed.
close_reason: Audit complete. Matrix in notes. Three options A/B/C presented to user for epic scope revision.

title: Epic: consolidate teacher-assignment flow under /management/teacher-assignment
description:
Consolidate teacher-assignment under /management/teacher-assignment with TWO modes:
- ?mode=by-grade (existing SubjectAssignmentTable + AssignmentFilters + TeacherSelector)
- ?mode=by-teacher (port of ClassroomResponsibility from legacy)

Driven by tz8 audit (closed): legacy is NOT fully redundant. Teacher-centric editor in /schedule/.../assign/teacher_responsibility has no canonical equivalent — full-replace UX faster for entering one teacher's full load.

Inbound legacy links:
- src/app/schedule/[academicYear]/[semester]/arrange/layout.tsx:67 empty-state CTA → /management/teacher-assignment?mode=by-grade&year=&semester=
- /schedule/.../assign → redirect → /management/teacher-assignment?mode=by-grade
- /schedule/.../assign/teacher_responsibility → redirect → /management/teacher-assignment?mode=by-teacher&teacherId=

Out of scope: redesigning either editor UX. Only consolidating their homes.

Phases:
- dn3 (revised): port teacher-centric editor + dependencies into /management/teacher-assignment with mode toggle
- lyw: redirects on /schedule/.../assign* routes
- 7xb: delete legacy components/hooks/modals
design:
Phased to avoid breaking arrange's empty-state gate and locked-schedule readers mid-flight.

Phase A — feature parity audit + port:
- Matrix: each legacy capability → does canonical have it? gap list.
- Port LockedScheduleList + useTeacherLockedSchedules into src/features/teaching-assignment/presentation/components/ (or src/features/lock if more natural).
- Port any QuickAssignmentPanel behavior not in SubjectAssignmentTable. Likely candidates: inline workload preview, classroom-grouped view.

Phase B — redirect inbound:
- arrange/layout.tsx CTA: href → /management/teacher-assignment?year=&semester=&gradeId=
- assign/teacher_responsibility back link → same.

Phase C — reduce legacy pages:
- /schedule/.../assign/page.tsx → redirect (mirror 2f398e12 pattern).
- /schedule/.../assign/teacher_responsibility/page.tsx → redirect.

Phase D — delete dead code:
- src/app/schedule/.../assign/component/ (ShowTeacherData, AddSubjectModal, SelectClassRoomModal, QuickAssignmentPanel)
- src/app/schedule/.../assign/components/ (LockedScheduleList after port)
- src/app/schedule/.../assign/hooks/ (useTeacherSchedule, useTeacherLockedSchedules after port)

Rejected alt: keep dual flow, route both through teacher-validation.service. Stops rule drift but doesn't remove duplication maintenance cost. Reject — same reasoning as 2f398e12 for config.
acceptance_criteria:
- Single canonical page (/management/teacher-assignment) hosts all teacher-assignment editing.
- All legacy routes redirect to canonical with query params preserving context.
- No remaining inbound link to /schedule/.../assign* except redirect destinations.
- Locked-schedule readout (or equivalent) accessible from canonical flow.
- All E2E specs that exercised legacy flow updated to canonical or deleted if obsolete.
- pnpm typecheck/lint/test/test:e2e green.

title: E2E: wizard copyAssignments toggle carries teacher assignments
description:
Follow-up to a77. Action + UI wiring landed with unit tests. Need Playwright E2E coverage for end-to-end carryover behavior.

Scope:
- Spec file under e2e/ covering CreateSemesterWizard.
- Two scenarios:
  1. Create new semester with copyFrom=<existing>, copyAssignments=true → navigate to /management/teacher-assignment for new year/sem → assignment rows visible without manual click.
  2. Create new semester with copyFrom=<existing>, copyAssignments=false → target page empty.
- Seed prerequisite: existing semester with teachers_responsibility rows (use seed or programmatic setup via test helper).

Acceptance:
- pnpm test:e2e green for new spec.
- Spec runs under pnpm dev:e2e (loads .env.test, hits local docker postgres :5433).
- No reliance on remote Prisma Accelerate.
notes: Depends on a77 implementation already merged. mapAssignmentsForTarget unit tests in src/features/teaching-assignment/domain/utils/copy-assignments.test.ts cover pure logic. CopyPreviousStep.test.tsx covers checkbox behavior. E2E proves transaction-level integration.
close_reason: E2E spec landed in e2e/26-wizard-copy-assignments.spec.ts; both scenarios green. Also fixed dashboard wiring (existingSemesters prop).

title: Add copy-assignments option to CreateSemesterWizard
description:
Wizard currently copies only timeslots/config when cloning previous semester. Caption on CopyPreviousStep explicitly states teacher assignments are NOT copied. Per-grade copy exists in /management/teacher-assignment via copyAssignmentsAction but requires admin to repeat per grade after semester creation.

Goal: optional all-grades carryover at semester creation time, default-off, opt-in toggle.

Scope:
1. Add copyAssignments boolean to CreateSemesterContext + CopyPreviousStep checkbox (default false).
2. Thread copyAssignments through createSemesterWithTimeslotsAction + createSemesterAction inputs.
3. In semester.actions.ts create flow, after timeslots copy, if copyAssignments=true: list all gradelevel IDs that have rows in source (TeacherID,GradeID,SubjectCode,AcademicYear,Semester) tuple → loop copyAssignmentsFromPreviousSemester per grade.
4. Update caption to reflect new toggle.
5. Surface count summary in wizard success state.

Notes:
- copyAssignmentsFromPreviousSemester signature: (sourceGradeId, sourceSem, sourceYear, targetGradeId, targetSem, targetYear). Same-grade mapping correct (gradelevel labels persist across years).
- bulkAssignTeachers upserts on (SubjectCode,GradeID,Semester,AcademicYear,TeacherID) — safe idempotent.
- handleCopyFromPrevious (TeacherAssignmentPage.tsx:40) prev-sem math reusable: SEM_1→prev SEM_2 prev year, SEM_2→prev SEM_1 same year.
- Workload validation (teacher-validation.service) NOT run on copy path. Decide: validate post-copy and warn, or accept that source was already valid.
design:
Default OFF preserves current safe behavior (staffing changes between semesters common — retired teachers, new subject mix). Toggle gives power-user shortcut without masking changes silently.

Reuse copyAssignmentsAction rather than new bulk variant: existing action revalidates /management/teacher-assignment + invalidates teachers/stats caches. Loop in server action; surface aggregate count.

Alternative considered: new bulkCopyAssignmentsForSemesterAction taking sourceConfigId+targetConfigId, single repository call iterating grades server-side. Cleaner but duplicates loop logic. Reject unless wizard adds rollback semantics (then transaction matters).
acceptance_criteria:
TDD-first: every production change preceded by failing test, verified RED before GREEN.

Test order (red before any prod code):
1. Unit (domain/repo): copyAssignmentsFromPreviousSemester loop semantics — given source has 2 grades × 3 subjects, target gets 6 rows with matching (GradeID, SubjectCode, TeacherID, TeachHour). Mock prisma only at boundary; prefer integration with test DB.
2. Unit: prev-sem math — SEM_1 target → SEM_2 prev year source; SEM_2 target → SEM_1 same year source.
3. Unit: idempotent re-run — running copy twice produces same row count, no duplicate (upsert via bulkAssignTeachers).
4. Unit: empty source → target unchanged, count=0, no error.
5. Action: createSemesterWithTimeslotsAction with copyAssignments=true threads through and returns aggregate count in result.
6. Action: copyAssignments=false (default) leaves teachers_responsibility empty for target.
7. Component: CopyPreviousStep renders checkbox only when copyFrom selected; default unchecked.
8. Component: caption text no longer claims teacher data uncopied unconditionally.
9. E2E (Playwright, pnpm dev:e2e): wizard flow with copyAssignments=true → /management/teacher-assignment for target year/sem shows rows without manual click.
10. E2E: wizard flow with copyAssignments=false → target page shows empty assignments.

Definition of done:
- Each box above has corresponding test committed BEFORE its prod change, with git log showing test commit preceding implementation commit (or single commit where test diff precedes impl diff and was verified to fail).
- pnpm test green. pnpm test:e2e green. pnpm typecheck green. pnpm lint green.
- Output pristine: no warnings, no console errors in E2E run.
notes:
TDD discipline reminder:
- Write test, run, watch FAIL with expected message. If pass on first run → testing existing behavior, fix test.
- Minimal GREEN code only. No options/flags not driven by a test.
- Refactor only when green. Don't add behavior in refactor.
- No mocks unless unavoidable. Prisma boundary OK to stub; domain logic uses real code.
- Skipping a test because "obvious" = rationalization. Iron law: no prod code without failing test first.
close_reason: Implementation + unit tests landed. E2E split to clr.

title: Happy-path publish e2e: dedicated minimal MOE-compliant seed mode + Playwright project
description: Split from z45 (block test shipped there). The clean-publish happy path (config flips DRAFT->PUBLISHED through the readiness gate with status=ready) cannot run against the demo seed: checkPublishReadiness + findFullConfigData use UNFILTERED queries (program.findMany({IsActive:true}), gradelevel.findMany()), so readiness is a GLOBAL property. All 18 seeded programs fail MOE on MISSING learning areas (M4/5/6-SCI have no ARTS/CAREER program_subject rows) + systemic under-crediting (juniors ~2.5 vs 5 required) — not a MinCredits bump. AND every gradelevel must be 100% scheduled in the term. So a green happy-path needs a dedicated minimal-seed MODE that TRUNCATEs and seeds a tiny compliant world, wired through a SEPARATE Playwright project so it doesn't break specs that expect the full 18-grade school.
design: Minimal compliant world: 1 admin; 1 program Year=4 Track=GENERAL (GENERAL skips validateTrackElectives — the only track with no elective-group requirement, so easiest to satisfy); ~10 subjects covering all 8 learning areas with MinCredits >= senior minimums (THAI3/MATH3/SCI3/SOCIAL2/HEALTH2/ARTS1/CAREER1/FOREIGN2); 1 gradelevel bound to that program; small timeslot grid (e.g. 5 non-break slots); class_schedule filling that grade 100%; teacher responsibilities + table grid for wizard lock gating (getWizardState: hasGrid && hasResponsibilities && hasPlacements). Then global readiness=ready. New Playwright project with its own globalSetup seeding this mode (env flag e.g. SEED_PUBLISH_HAPPY=true). Test: /schedule/<yr>/<sem>/lock -> expand readiness-accordion-summary -> chip 'พร้อมเผยแพร่' -> open-publish-dialog-btn -> title 'ยืนยันการเผยแพร่' -> confirm-publish-btn -> 'เผยแพร่ตารางสำเร็จ' -> config-status-badge PUBLISHED.
acceptance_criteria: e2e test deterministically publishes a ready config to PUBLISHED through the readiness gate UI, isolated from the full-school specs.
close_reason: Shipped isolated publish-happy suite (seed-publish-happy.ts + playwright.config.publish-happy.ts + 25-publish-happy.spec.ts). 2 passed.

Clicking step 1 in the stepper leaves the wizard entirely with no in-stepper way back. Config grid editing was consolidated into /dashboard (pre-existing redirect). OPPORTUNISTIC FIX while in lock.actions.ts: wrap createBulkLocksAction (lock.actions.ts:144-187) in withPrismaTransaction so the existing '// Use transaction for atomicity' comment becomes true — currently false, a partial-failure bug.

description: After d809671f (config page read-only + Edit dialog, 2026-04-28), 18-admin-regressions.spec.ts:42-70 looks for 'ตั้งค่าตาราง' button on dashboard that opened an inline config dialog. Also 18-admin-regressions.spec.ts:93-106 (arrange time-row) may be valid but needs data; verify with seeded DB before unskipping.
close_reason: ADM-REG-001 rewritten: now navigates to config page, clicks แก้ไขตั้งค่า, edits time, cancels, re-opens to verify no persistence. ADM-REG-002 already has conditional skip guard.

title: Update or archive stale plans in docs/plans
description: docs/plans/2026-02-24-full-quality-pass-plan.md describes 35 P0 bugs already fixed in commits 94735861, 412b8f93. Plan not updated post-execution — misleads future readers. Sweep all docs/plans/*.md, mark completed plans as Done with commit refs, archive or delete fully shipped plans. prisma/migrations/PREVIEW_auth_migration.sql also lingering preview migration to clean.
close_reason: Deleted all 17 Feb-Mar 2026 plan files (fully executed post-launch quality pass). PREVIEW_auth_migration.sql was only in the dropped stash, never committed.

title: Clean up 35 lint warnings (hooks deps, unused vars, console.log)
description: 0 errors but 35 warnings: 7 react-hooks/exhaustive-deps, 6 unused-vars, 1 no-unsafe-function-type (Counter.tsx), 12 stray console.log in feature code, plus misc. Sweep file-by-file. Lower CI noise floor so future warnings stand out.
close_reason: All 24 lint warnings resolved: 0 errors 0 warnings. SelectMultipleTimeSlot refactored to useMemo, ProfilePage dead EmailSection removed, 12 other files cleaned.

value: RSC pitfall (Next16 + MUI): <Button component={Link}> (or any MUI component prop = a component reference) inside a SERVER component crashes at RUNTIME with 'Functions cannot be passed directly to Client Components' — RSC can't serialize the function. pnpm build does NOT catch it; only hit on navigation/e2e. Fix: use a plain href string (Button renders an anchor) or extract a 'use client' wrapper. Bit us in curriculum step page (commit d07e4eb9). component={Link} is fine inside client components.

test env: .env contains PRISMA_DATABASE_URL/ACCELERATE_URL pointing at remote Prisma Postgres. prisma/seed.ts uses 'import dotenv/config' which auto-loads .env even under 'dotenv -e .env.test'. src/lib/prisma.ts prismaClientSingleton prefers Accelerate URL over DATABASE_URL when set. .env.test and .env.test.local now explicitly set PRISMA_DATABASE_URL= and ACCELERATE_URL= empty to override .env and force the local docker postgres on :5433. If you ever see seed fail with USER_ALREADY_EXISTS while docker timetable-test-db shows User table empty, suspect this leak again.
